commit.message:
fix: PWA login scope, video upload corruption, CAE form responsiveness

- Move PWA meta tags from terrain layout to root layout metadata API
  so Safari sees them on /login (standalone mode works)
- Fix video upload: skip File double-wrapping when compressImage returns
  original file unchanged (compressed === file check)
- Make CAE/VT form responsive: sidebar collapses to horizontal scroll
  on mobile, wizard grids stack vertically on small screens

feat: offline file uploads with image/video compression (Session 4)

Photos compressed via Canvas API (5MB→300KB), videos capped at 1Mbps
bitrate (15MB→6MB). Files queue in IDB when offline, sync via FormData
through Axios on reconnect.

- compress-image.ts: Canvas API compression (max 1920px, JPEG 80%)
- offline-cae-service.ts: uploadFile, syncFileUpload, storage quota check
- cae-tab.tsx: handleFileUpload uses offlineCaeService (online + offline)

MEDIUM:
- hot page: win_rate/roi/followers || 0 → ?? null/0 (null win_rate was silently becoming 0)

commit.message:
fix: 모바일 웹 레이아웃·저장·진동·Alert 문제 수정

1. 레이아웃: dvh CSS 주입 + viewport-fit=cover 적용으로 모바일
   브라우저 주소창/하단바 짤림 해결. useSafeAreaInsets로 탭바
   높이 동적 계산 (safe-area-inset-bottom 적용).

2. 진동: alarmHelper의 vibrate()에서 Platform.OS === 'web'일 때
   즉시 return하여 웹에서 진동 호출 무시.

3. 저장: AsyncStorage 대신 Platform별 storage 유틸(src/utils/storage.ts)
   도입. 웹에서는 window.localStorage를 직접 사용하여 운동 삭제,
   루틴 저장 등 모든 데이터 쓰기/읽기 동작 보장.

4. Alert: 웹에서 네이티브 Alert.alert 대신 커스텀 모달
   (WebAlertModal) 사용. showAlert 유틸(src/utils/alert.ts)로
   모든 스크린에서 통합 호출.

commit.message:
fix: guard CLEU registration against combat lockdown + handle secret amounts

Part 1 - Registration (Outgoing_Detect.lua):
Frame:RegisterEvent("COMBAT_LOG_EVENT_UNFILTERED") is ADDON_ACTION_FORBIDDEN
while InCombatLockdown() is true (e.g. /reload inside a dungeon). Guard
_tryRegisterCLEU() with InCombatLockdown() and listen to PLAYER_REGEN_ENABLED
so registration retries as soon as combat drops. Store raw CLEU amounts
(no tonumber) since arithmetic on secret values would fail. Wrap isCrit
in SafeBool() for the same reason.

Part 2 - Secret values (Outgoing_Probe.lua):
In restricted content (M+, raids) CLEU amounts are secret numbers: arithmetic
throws but tostring() works via Blizzard metamethod. Add TryReadAmount() which
pcalls `raw + 0` to detect secret values. When secret: skip all threshold /
throttle comparisons and build display text via tostring(raw). When readable:
full threshold / overheal arithmetic as before.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Route all external fetches through server-side proxy

Replace slow, unreliable third-party CORS proxies (corsproxy.io,
allorigins.win) with a /api/proxy endpoint on our own backend.

- server.js: add /api/proxy with in-memory caching, host allowlist,
  and _cb param stripping so cache-busted Yahoo URLs hit cache correctly
- config.js: PROXIES and CHART_PROXIES now point to /api/proxy?url=
- overview.js: replace 3 hardcoded CORS proxy fallbacks with /api/proxy

TTLs: 30s for live prices (Yahoo/Finnhub), 60s for crypto, 5min for RSS.
Fixes: crypto not loading, overview charts missing, equity analysis slow.

commit.message:
Fix assistant chat crash: upgrade FastAPI and Pydantic for by_alias compatibility

FastAPI 0.109.0 passes by_alias=None during response serialization,
which newer pydantic-core rejects with "NoneType cannot be converted
to PyBool". Bumping to FastAPI 0.115.6 and Pydantic 2.10.3 resolves
the incompatibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add SchemaReviewSkill and SchemaReviewAgent for LinkML schema coverage audit

Implements comprehensive schema review system to identify validation gaps
and manual reimplementations across the codebase.

Features:
- SchemaReviewSkill: AST-based static analysis to discover schemas, scan
  validation usage, and identify data I/O points without validation
- SchemaReviewAgent: Orchestrates skill + ValidateLinkMLSkill integration,
  generates JSON + Markdown remediation roadmap with prioritized findings
- 28/28 tests passing (15 skill tests + 13 agent tests)
- Integrated via `just review-schema` command
- Discovers 6 schemas, identifies 210 findings (9 errors, 195 warnings, 6 info)

Findings on MicroGrowAgents codebase:
- 9 manual reimplementations in schema_validation.py (should use linkml-validate)
- 195 unvalidated I/O points across database/, skills/, agents/, scripts/

commit.message:
feat(#293): implement scaling policy engine with configurable thresholds (#337)

Adds internal/scaling package with:
- ThresholdPolicy: evaluates pool + system snapshots and returns a
  Decision (ScaleUp/ScaleDown/Hold) with delta, reason, and confidence
- PolicyConfig: fully configurable via YAML — min/max workers,
  cooldown, evaluation interval, scale-up memory ceiling and queue
  trigger, scale-down idle threshold and duration
- NotifyScaled(): cooldown resets when autoscaler applies a decision
- DefaultPolicyConfig(): conservative defaults (max 8 workers, 2m
  cooldown, scale up at queue >= 2 + all busy, scale down at >= 2
  idle workers for >= 2 minutes)
- Memory pressure computed as heap/sys ratio; CPU not available from
  Go runtime metrics (autoscaler can extend later via gopsutil)
- 21 unit tests covering all acceptance criteria

commit.message:
perf: parallelize k-means++ initialization with rayon

The k-means++ init loop was fully sequential, scanning all data points
with EMD distance k times (once per centroid). For large datasets like
600-bucket turn clustering (~millions of points × 600 centroids), this
burned ~1 hour on a single core while all other cores sat idle.

Replace the sequential inner loop with par_iter + par_iter_mut to
parallelize EMD computation and min-distance updates across all cores.

commit.message:
Optimize slice processing with read-ahead and LZ4 compression (#357)

* Use LZ4 compression for worker-to-consumer Arrow IPC transfers

Switch the two Arrow IPC write sites in _surface_unpack_reconstruct.py
from compression="uncompressed" to compression="lz4". LZ4 is the
fastest Arrow-supported codec, adding negligible CPU overhead while
meaningfully reducing /tmp file sizes for the inter-process data
transfer of exploded slices and the prepared DataFrame.

https://claude.ai/code/session_019CT1m8WvSWacPb5bESVxyM

* Add readahead for slice I/O in _build_records_chunked

Use a ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1) to pre-read the next Arrow IPC
slice in a background thread while the current slice is being processed
by the C++ trie-building extension (which releases the GIL). This
overlaps disk I/O + LZ4 decompression + numpy conversion with the
CPU-bound extend_tree_searchtable_cpp_from_exploded call.

Also extract _read_slice helper to encapsulate the Arrow IPC read +
numpy conversion logic.

https://claude.ai/code/session_019CT1m8WvSWacPb5bESVxyM

* Release GIL during C++ trie insertion and batch progress updates

Add py::gil_scoped_release around the insert_artifact hot path in
extend_trie_searchtable_exploded so the readahead thread can perform
I/O truly in parallel with the CPU-bound trie building.

Batch 100 artifact insertions per GIL release/reacquire cycle to
amortize the cost of the pbar Python callback, reducing GIL
contention overhead.

https://claude.ai/code/session_019CT1m8WvSWacPb5bESVxyM

* Use AtomicCounter for GIL-free tqdm progress during trie insertion

Replace the ProgressBar Python callback in extend_trie_searchtable_exploded
with a C++ AtomicCounter that can be incremented without holding the GIL.
The entire insertion loop now runs with the GIL released.

On the Python side, a daemon polling thread reads the atomic counter
every 100ms and drives tqdm.update() calls, giving live progress bar
updates without any GIL contention on the hot path.

https://claude.ai/code/session_019CT1m8WvSWacPb5bESVxyM

* Move progress bar polling thread into C++ for GIL-free tqdm updates

Instead of exposing AtomicCounter to Python and managing a polling thread
there, the C++ side now spawns a std::thread that sleeps for 10-second
intervals, reads the atomic counter, acquires the GIL, and calls
pbar.update(). The main insertion loop runs entirely without the GIL.

The progress thread is joined while the GIL is released to avoid
deadlock with the thread's final gil_scoped_acquire.

https://claude.ai/code/session_019CT1m8WvSWacPb5bESVxyM

* Extract ProgressPoller class with condvar for instant completion

Address PR review feedback:
- Extract progress thread into ProgressPoller class next to ProgressBar
- Use condition_variable so poller wakes immediately when counter
  reaches total (no 10s wait on completion)
- Remove separate done flag; poller exits when counter >= total
- Merge ProgressBar operator() overloads into single default n=1
- Keep insertion loop structure similar to original
- Update pbar unconditionally (even if delta is 0)
- Add log_context_duration to _read_slice for per-load-op logging

https://claude.ai/code/session_019CT1m8WvSWacPb5bESVxyM

* Remove unused collections import from _surface_unpack_reconstruct

https://claude.ai/code/session_019CT1m8WvSWacPb5bESVxyM

* Fix black formatting in _surface_unpack_reconstruct

https://claude.ai/code/session_019CT1m8WvSWacPb5bESVxyM

* Address PR review: remove readahead, const u64, clean up GIL scope

- Remove concurrent.futures readahead (benchmarked as no-op)
- Use simple enumerate(slices) loop instead
- Add const to u64 parameters in ProgressBar/ProgressPoller
- Add scope comment and remove indentation inside GIL release block

https://claude.ai/code/session_019CT1m8WvSWacPb5bESVxyM

* Refactor readahead into _readahead_slices generator, log numpy conversions

- Extract readahead logic into a generator helper that prefetches the
  next slice while the caller processes the current one
- Restore per-column to_numpy() logging with log_context_duration
- Keep const u64 and GIL scope comment changes from prior review

https://claude.ai/code/session_019CT1m8WvSWacPb5bESVxyM

* Add scope comment for ProgressPoller block

https://claude.ai/code/session_019CT1m8WvSWacPb5bESVxyM

* Restore original for-loop structure and note thread-safe logging

- Revert while/for loop back to original for/for pattern per review
- Add docstring note that Python logging is thread-safe for worker thread

https://claude.ai/code/session_019CT1m8WvSWacPb5bESVxyM

* Remove unnecessary docstring note about thread-safe logging

https://claude.ai/code/session_019CT1m8WvSWacPb5bESVxyM

* Rename np_array to np_arrays

https://claude.ai/code/session_019CT1m8WvSWacPb5bESVxyM

* Put unlinking log call back on one line

https://claude.ai/code/session_019CT1m8WvSWacPb5bESVxyM

* Change import to 'from concurrent import futures'

https://claude.ai/code/session_019CT1m8WvSWacPb5bESVxyM

* Fix slices type hint from Iterator to Sequence

len() and indexing are used on slices, so Sequence is correct.

https://claude.ai/code/session_019CT1m8WvSWacPb5bESVxyM

* Revert slices type hint to Iterator, materialize with list()

slices is a lazy iterator from workers; materialize it inside
_build_records_chunked before passing to _readahead_slices which
needs indexing.

https://claude.ai/code/session_019CT1m8WvSWacPb5bESVxyM

* Use iterator-based _readahead_slices, keep nslices via give_len

Rewrite _readahead_slices to consume an iterator instead of
requiring sequence indexing. Keep len(slices) since the caller
wraps the pool.imap result with give_len.

https://claude.ai/code/session_019CT1m8WvSWacPb5bESVxyM

* Apply suggestion from @mmore500

* Fix malformed IPC file error by disabling LZ4 compression on prepared arrow temp file

Polars 1.37.1 hits a decompression length mismatch when workers
read partial slices from an LZ4-compressed IPC file via scan_ipc.
Using uncompressed IPC avoids the issue — compression is unnecessary
for this short-lived temp file.

https://claude.ai/code/session_019CT1m8WvSWacPb5bESVxyM

* Add comment referencing polars issue #26316 for IPC compression workaround

https://claude.ai/code/session_019CT1m8WvSWacPb5bESVxyM

* Restore LZ4 compression, exclude polars 1.37.* to avoid IPC scan+slice bug

polars 1.37.x has a regression where scan_ipc + slice + collect on
LZ4-compressed IPC files produces decompression length mismatches
(https://github.com/pola-rs/polars/issues/26316). Excluding 1.37.*
resolves to 1.36.1 which predates the bug. Removes prior !=1.38.1
exclusion. phyloframe 0.1.0 still blocks 1.38.1 independently.

https://claude.ai/code/session_019CT1m8WvSWacPb5bESVxyM

---------

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Session 2B: Hardware-verified emotion sync on Waveshare AMOLED 1.32

Built and flashed xiaozhi-esp32 with Mochi emotion hooks on real hardware.
8 timing test runs confirm GIF_LOAD triggers in 0ms after EMOTION_TAG.
Protocol discovery: emotion arrives 220-330ms after STATE:speaking (server
behavior). Fixed PRId64 nano-printf issue (%lld cast). Added placeholder
GIF generator tool.

Verified: boot, state logging, emotion mapping, emoji fallback,
GIF_MISSING graceful handling, heap stability (87-126KB free).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: 5-word vocabulary (1 verb + 4 nouns), images, verb auto-save & profile 'Outro' input

- generateHook now requests exactly 5 words: 1 verb (verbWord) + 4 nouns
- upsertVocabularyItem stores imageUrl and wordType fields
- finishLesson passes imageUrl/wordType per word and pre-caches verb conjugation
- VocabCard shows 72×72 image thumbnail (placeholder for items without image)
- Verbs page shows "Verbos aprendidos" chips for user's learned verbs
- Profile page shows custom text input when "Outro" profession is selected

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: state record legend on athlete view page

Adds a red-accented legend bar above athlete cards showing the state
record Squat / Bench / Deadlift weights (and record holder name) for
the selected weight class. Gender is auto-detected from the spreadsheet
title; records are loaded from the shared state-records-data.js.

commit.message:
add --plan-only flag to /tdd for plan-and-ticket-only workflows

Allows running CLARIFY → DESIGN → PLAN → TICKETS without entering the
build phase. Exits with a summary of artifacts. Resume later with
/resume <feature> to start building.
